name: Swiss Almond Macaroons
recipeInstructions:
1. Process almonds and 3 tablespoons sugar in a food processor bowl fitted with the metal blade, pulsing on and off until very finely ground.
2. Add vanilla extract and pulse again. Scrape inside of the work bowl with a metal spatula.
3. Add water and pulse again to mix.
4. Leave almond mixture in the work bowl while preparing the meringue.
5. Whisk egg whites and 1/2 cup sugar in a mixer bowl. Place the bowl over a pan of simmering water and whisk until the egg whites are hot and the sugar is dissolved, about 2 minutes.
6. Whip the meringue until cooled and fluffy.
7. Add about 1/4 of the meringue to the work bowl containing almond mixture and pulse to mix.
8. Add another 1/4 of meringue to the work bowl and pulse again.
9. Remove the blade and scrape the almond-meringue mixture into a pastry bag fitted with a plain tube nozzle that has a 1/4-inch opening.
10. Pipe 24 (1/2-inch) macaroons on each pan (48 total), leaving 1 inch between macaroons.
11. Bake macaroons at 375°F until lightly golden, about 15 minutes. Switch pans from back to front and top to bottom once or twice during baking.
12. Cool macaroons on the pans.
13. When cool, remove macarons from parchment paper.
14. Bring cream to boil in a saucepan. Remove from heat. Add chocolate and allow to stand for 2 minutes. Whisk smooth.
15. Scrape filling into a bowl to cool.
16. Assemble by placing a dab of chocolate filling on the flat side of each baked macaron. Press the flat sides of the remaining macarons against the filling.
17. Store in a cool place. Serve macaroons on the day they are prepared. Makes about 2 dozen.
